prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF backend, density-fit RI), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz with no re-optimization. The N±1 states used cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Atom-condensed Mulliken partial charges were used throughout.

Headline result: the most electrophilic atom is C5 (atom index 5, 1-based), with f⁺ = 1.9242931 — the largest Fukui-plus value across all 9 atoms. The most nucleophilic atom is also C5, with f⁻ = 0.1616371.

Full per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺ / f⁻ / f⁰ / dual descriptor):
  Atom 1  O:  −1.3316182 / 0.08382155 / −0.62389834 / −1.4154398

  Atom 6  H:  −1.2190639 / 0.10248412 / −0.55828991 / −1.3215481
  Atom 7  H:  −1.2191056 / 0.10251350 / −0.55829603 / −1.3216191
  Atom 8  H:  −0.76678308 / 0.10481615 / −0.33098346 / −0.87159923
  Atom 9  H:  −0.76674059 / 0.10479266 / −0.33097396 / −0.87153325

Integrity checks all passed (trustworthy: true). Both f⁺ and f⁻ sum-conservation checks returned 1.0000. All three integrity check items are reproduced verbatim in the warnings field above.
